claim: WHO Surveillance System for Attacks on Healthcare documented over 1500 attacks in 2023, with majority in Ukraine, Palestine, Sudan, and Myanmar, but facility-level service disruption data remains fragmentary.

source.title: Surveillance System for Attacks on Health Care

method: Reviewed WHO SSA annual report and attack statistics.
limitations: Attack reporting depends on verification access, which is limited in active conflict zones.
Attack count does not measure service disruption directly.

claim: A 2022 Lancet study found conflict-zone facilities within 25 km of armed clashes experienced 30-60 percent reductions in outpatient visits and 40-80 percent reductions in maternal health services, with DHIS2 data enabling utilization tracking.

source.title: Health service disruption in conflict-affected settings: a multi-country analysis

method: Reviewed facility-utilization analysis, conflict-proximity methodology, and service-disruption estimates.
limitations: DHIS2 data completeness varies during conflict.
Proximity-based analysis may misclassify facilities not directly in conflict zones.
